clinical_coherence.notes: 24F with viral prodrome followed by psychiatric symptoms, seizures, orofacial dyskinesias, autonomic instability, and choreoathetoid movements. This is the textbook NMDAR encephalitis phenotype in a young woman. Vitals (tachycardia 114, BP lability, low-grade fever 38.4) reflect autonomic instability. Hyponatremia (SIADH) is an expected complication. All elements form a flawless clinical picture.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG shows extreme delta brush (pathognomonic) with electrographic seizures. MRI shows bilateral medial temporal FLAIR hyperintensity without hemorrhage or DWI restriction (favoring autoimmune over HSV). CSF shows lymphocytic pleocytosis (59 cells), oligoclonal bands, elevated IgG index, and positive anti-NMDAR antibodies at 1:320. All outputs perfectly concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G04.81 correct. Critical actions include LP for NMDAR antibodies, empiric acyclovir, immunotherapy, pelvic imaging for teratoma, and continuous EEG. Contraindicated actions (antipsychotics alone, brain biopsy) are clinically sound. Ovarian teratoma found on follow-up ultrasound is an excellent detail.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic presentation with all cardinal features: young woman, viral prodrome, psychiatric-seizure-dyskinesia-autonomic tetrad. Extreme delta brush on EEG and positive antibodies make diagnosis straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 24-year-old Caucasian female graduate student is the classic demographic for NMDAR encephalitis. Highly realistic.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case covering the full NMDAR encephalitis phenotype, extreme delta brush EEG pattern, the importance of teratoma screening, empiric acyclovir pending HSV PCR, and stepwise immunotherapy approach. The follow-up outputs (teratoma US, autoimmune panel, CT body, repeat LP, rituximab screening) are comprehensive.

strengths: Textbook NMDAR encephalitis phenotype in classic demographic
Extreme delta brush on EEG is a key teaching pattern
Comprehensive follow-up outputs including teratoma discovery
Well-constructed differential diagnosis with clear distinguishing features
Multiple follow-up outputs provide depth for treatment planning
